name: 319K handheld mario kart
description: Recreated Nintendo's 1992 Super Mario Kart's mode 7 graphics on the TM4C123 microcontroller.Implemented and mounted an SD breakoutboard to expand memory for music. Used UART communication between two microcontrollers.

name: arduino nano e2 training board
description: This is a PCB made so that students at UT enigneering program could learn surface mounted soldering, and have a programmable microcontroller at the end of training.

name: UT SVT Current Driver and SOC
description: Configured ADC on STM32f4 microcontroller to read data from two hall effect sensors. Used current readings to manage electrical current for UT Solar vehical team's BPS system. Implemented coulomb counted to calculate state of charge.

name: Physical Therapy foot sensor
description: This pcb was meant to use the TI MSP430 microcontroller and Silicon Lab's BGM123 bluetooth module to send presure measurements on the heals of recovering physical therapy patients to help them recover.

name: e-Nable Phoenix prosthetic remake
description: Remodeled e-Nable's Phoenix prosthetic hand in Fusion 360. Created a parametric workflow and lookup tables to automatically resize the hand for e-Nable's large volunteer group.
